purpose.title: Purpose
purpose.body:
## Purpose

Use this protocol when the user asks to update, refresh, reinstall, or pull the latest SocratexAI pipeline in an already configured project.

The agent must know where the pipeline comes from and which updater to run before changing files.

source_resolution.title: Source Resolution
source_resolution.body:
## Source Resolution

Determine the update source in this order:

1. `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json` value `pipeline.update_source`.
2. `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json` value `pipeline.public_bootstrap_url`.
3. The installed `SocratexAI/PUBLIC-BOOTSTRAP.md` source URL if documented by the project.
4. A user-provided URL or local source path.
5. Ask the user for the update source.

Do not guess a repository URL when no source is configured.

update_command.title: Update Command
update_command.body:
## Update Command

Cheap update is the default. A normal update should only refresh the managed `SocratexAI/` package, keep project configuration and project memory untouched, refresh the compact instance feature list, update root directives, and run the cheap feature-contract check.

For public user updates, prefer a Git source when possible:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/update_pipeline_from_link.ps1 -Source "https://github.com/Socratex/SocratexAI.git" -SourceMode Git
```

A zip or local checkout is still valid:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/update_pipeline_from_link.ps1 -Source "<zip-or-local-source>"
```

Do not pass `-ReinitializeNew` by default. Use it only when the update explicitly introduces new missing-only initialized artifacts that should be created in the project root. When this is needed, the updater invokes SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/reinitialize_pipeline.ps1 in missing-only mode.

Do not pass `-FullVerify` by default. Use it when the user has enough budget/time or when debugging the update itself; it rebuilds compiled knowledge and runs full `audit_docs.ps1` after the cheap update.

Use the selected project packs from config when reinitialization is explicitly requested.

If `pipeline.update_command` exists in config, use it as the command template after replacing `<source>` with the resolved update source, but preserve the cheap default unless the config explicitly opts into full verification.

Reinitialization must be missing-only: create newly introduced files or config defaults, but do not overwrite active project memory.
Pipeline package update is a managed mirror sync, not a loose copy. The updater must mirror source-owned package paths under `SocratexAI/`, including root routing catalogs such as `DOCS.json`, `WORKFLOW.json`, `COMMANDS.json`, `FLOWS.json`, `SCRIPTS.json`, and source context such as `context-docs/`, and remove obsolete files inside those managed paths before copying the new source. This preserves structural changes such as `tools/<category>/` and prevents stale flat scripts, old templates, or dead general documents from surviving after an update.

The updater must not overwrite project-owned configuration or memory such as root `PIPELINE-CONFIG.json`, active state, plans, backlog, decisions, or project context docs. Source-owned files inside `SocratexAI/` are replaceable; project-owned files outside `SocratexAI/` are not.

When updating the pipeline inside Riftbound Vanguard, ask whether the user also wants the source `SocratexPipeline` repository upgraded and pushed when the installed pipeline change exposes a reusable pipeline improvement.

For maintainer-only upgrades from a private reference project, use the maintainer upgrade tool only when the user explicitly requests that source:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/upgrade_from_riftbound.ps1 -RiftboundPath "<path>"
```

For migration from another AI pipeline, use:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File SocratexAI/tools/pipeline/migrate_ai_pipeline.ps1
```

feature_learning.title: Feature Learning
feature_learning.body:
## Feature Learning

`pipeline_featurelist.json` is the compact feature-id contract for comparing source and installed pipelines.

The source `SocratexPipeline/pipeline_featurelist.json` is canonical and must not reference specific installed projects.
It uses the canonical JSON list-document shape: root `index`, `content`, and `metadata`.

`pipeline_featurelist.json` uses a two-layer contract inside `content`:

- `content.features` stays as the cheap comparison index for source/instance diffs.
- `content.feature_contracts` is the artifact-level contract used to prove a feature is actually synchronized.

`metadata` stores schema, pipeline identity, role, update date, comparison rules, allowed sync directions, and compatibility notes.
Readers may accept older root `features` and root `feature_contracts` only as migration input; source output should stay `index/content/metadata`.

Every source feature must define `summary`, `required_paths`, `required_scripts`, `required_catalog_entries`, `required_docs`, `sync_direction`, `promotion_checklist`, `verification_commands`, and `known_failure_if_missing`.

Run the contract checker before promoting or publishing source changes:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/check_pipeline_feature_contracts.ps1
```

When passing changed paths, the checker also verifies that every changed pipeline-owned artifact is assigned to at least one feature contract:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/check_pipeline_feature_contracts.ps1 -Paths tools/pipeline/example.ps1,SCRIPTS.json
```

Installed projects should keep an instance manifest at project root in the same list-document shape with:

- `content.features`
- `content.comparison_to_source.same`
- `content.comparison_to_source.missing_from_instance`
- `content.comparison_to_source.extra_in_instance`

When a project develops a reusable non-project-specific pipeline behavior, inspect it with:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/learn_pipeline_features.ps1 -ProjectPath "<project>"
```

For structured local intake that an agent can read later, generate a JSON learning report with:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/report_pipeline_learning.ps1 -ProjectPath "<project>" -OutputPath "learning/inbox/<project>.json"
```

For public pipeline improvement intake, use only a prefilled GitHub Issue URL:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/open_pipeline_learning_issue.ps1 -ProjectPath "<project>" -Open
```

This is the only supported public network intake path for now. It does not require a GitHub token in the pipeline and does not write through an API; the user reviews the prefilled issue and submits it from the browser.

When the report comes from a concrete AI improvement, include a short explanation and changed scripts:

```powershell
powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File tools/repo/open_pipeline_learning_issue.ps1 -ProjectPath "<project>" -LearnedSummary "<what AI learned>" -ChangedScripts @("tools/example.ps1") -Open
```

Apply selected reusable features to the source manifest with `-Apply -IncludeFeatures <ids>` or `-Apply -AcceptAll` only after excluding project/domain-specific features and confirming that each selected feature has a `content.feature_contracts` entry. The promotion tool refuses selected feature IDs that do not carry a contract.
Feature learning is not complete when only the feature ID is copied. A promoted reusable feature must also bring every required artifact: scripts, script category placement, SCRIPTS.json path and input/output contracts, general documents, templates, compiled-context source lists, workflow/audit/update rules, and migration or cleanup logic. If the child proves a structural pipeline behavior, port the source artifacts first, then update the feature list.

Pipeline-owned improvements under `tools/`, `core/`, `project/`, `templates/`, or `adapters/` must update `pipeline_featurelist.json`; `tools/repo/check_pipeline_featurelist_update.ps1` enforces this only for pipeline-owned changed paths, so ordinary project tasks do not need feature-list edits.

After the source learns new canonical feature IDs, future project updates and reinitialization should run `tools/repo/sync_pipeline_featurelist.ps1` so installed projects can compare themselves against the new source feature list.

workflow.title: Workflow
workflow.body:
## Workflow

1. Read root `SOCRATEX.md`.
2. Read installed pipeline config.
3. Resolve the update source.
4. Check runtime availability or follow `core/SCRIPT-FALLBACK.json`.
5. Run the updater with explicit source and packs; verify that managed `SocratexAI/` paths were mirror-synced, including deletion of obsolete source-owned files.
6. Reinitialize missing-only only when explicitly requested with `-ReinitializeNew`.
7. Run `SocratexAI/tools/repo/check_pipeline_feature_contracts.ps1` when available.
8. Run `SocratexAI/tools/documents/audit_docs.ps1` only when `-FullVerify` is requested or a cheap check fails.
9. Run `SocratexAI/core/ACTIVATION-CHECK.json` after update when the user is validating setup, not for every low-budget update.
10. Report updated source, packs, whether full verification was skipped, and remaining risks.

safety.title: Safety
safety.body:
## Safety

Do not overwrite project memory such as active state, branch state, plans, backlog, decisions, or context docs unless the updater explicitly preserves or migrates them.

Do not update from an untrusted URL without telling the user what source will be used.

If update source is missing, ask for it and stop before changing files.
